revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 大綱金刀比羅神社 |画像= {{画像募集中|cat=横浜市}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} |所在地 = [[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[神奈川区]][[台町 (横浜市)|台町]]7-34 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[大物主神]]、[[金山彦神]]、[[日本武尊]]、[[大山祇神]] |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 伝[[1180年]]（飯綱大権現/大綱神社）
[[1642年]]（金毘羅大権現）[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html 由緒] - 大綱金刀比羅神社 |本殿 = |別名 = 横浜のこんぴらさん[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 大綱金刀比羅神社] - 神奈川県神社庁 |札所等 = |例祭 = 5月16～17日 |神事 = }} '''大綱金刀比羅神社'''（おおつなことひらじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[神奈川区]][[台町 (横浜市)|台町]]にある[[神社]]。 == 概要 == 元々は山上の「[[飯綱大権現]]」と麓の「[[金毘羅大権現]]」が別々に鎮座しており、前者には[[源頼朝]]にまつわる[[1180年]]（[[治承]]4年）の創建伝説が、後者には[[1642年]]（[[寛永]]19年）の[[棟札]]がある。 明治時代の神仏分離によってそれぞれ「大綱神社」「金刀比羅神社」となり、後者は[[1882年]]（明治15年）に村社に列せられる。[[1911年]]（明治44年）、前年の裏山崩壊によって半壊した金刀比羅神社の社殿再建を機に、大綱神社を合祀して「大綱金刀比羅神社」とする。 [[1985年]]（昭和60年）、裏山が崩壊して本殿・拝殿を喪失、神楽殿を「仮社殿」として現在に至る[https://dailyportalz.jp/kiji/shrine-with-tengu-and-cave-near-Yokohama-station 横浜駅から歩いて約10分 天狗と洞窟が迎える神社] - [[デイリーポータルZ]] 2022/11/29。 == 祭神 == * [[大物主神]] * [[金山彦神]] * [[日本武尊]] * [[大山祇神]] ; 内神（習合時代の祭神） * [[船霊]]神 * [[猿田彦]]尊 * [[不動明王]] * [[十一面観音]] * [[烏天狗]] * [[飯綱権現]] * [[大日如来]] == 境内 == * 天狗彫刻 * 洞窟 ; 境内社 * [[荒神]]社 * 横浜[[弁財天]] * [[稲荷社]] * [[龍神社]] == 祭事 == * 1月 - [[歳旦祭]]、初金刀比羅祭 * 2月 - 稲荷祭 * 5月16日～17日 - 例大祭　 * 6月 - [[大祓]]夏越祭 * 7月 - [[七夕祭]] * 12月 - 十日納、金刀比羅祭、大祓除夜祭 == 交通アクセス == * [[京急]][[神奈川駅]]より徒歩3分。 * JR[[横浜駅]]西口より徒歩5分 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == *[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/ 大綱金刀比羅神社] - 公式サイト {{DEFAULTSORT:おおつなことひらしんしや}} [[Category:横浜市の神社]] [[Category:神奈川区の歴史]] [[Category:神奈川区の建築物]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧村社]] [[Category:金刀比羅神社]]

[[1642年]]（金毘羅大権現）[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html 由緒] - 大綱金刀比羅神社 |本殿 = |別名 = 横浜のこんぴらさん[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 大綱金刀比羅神社] - 神奈川県神社庁 |札所等 = |例祭 = 5月16～17日 |神事 = }} '''大綱金刀比羅神社'''（おおつなことひらじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[神奈川区]][[台町 (横浜市)|台町]]にある[[神社]]。 == 概要 == 元々は山上の「[[飯綱大権現]]」と麓の「[[金毘羅大権現]]」が別々に鎮座しており、前者には[[源頼朝]]にまつわる[[1180年]]（[[治承]]4年）の創建伝説が、後者には[[1642年]]（[[寛永]]19年）の[[棟札]]がある。 明治時代の神仏分離によってそれぞれ「大綱神社」「金刀比羅神社」となり、後者は[[1882年]]（明治15年）に村社に列せられる。[[1911年]]（明治44年）、前年の裏山崩壊によって半壊した金刀比羅神社の社殿再建を機に、大綱神社を合祀して「大綱金刀比羅神社」とする。 [[1985年]]（昭和60年）、裏山が崩壊して本殿・拝殿を喪失、神楽殿を「仮社殿」として現在に至る[https://dailyportalz.jp/kiji/shrine-with-tengu-and-cave-near-Yokohama-station 横浜駅から歩いて約10分 天狗と洞窟が迎える神社] - [[デイリーポータルZ]] 2022/11/29。 == 祭神 == * [[大物主神]] * [[金山彦神]] * [[日本武尊]] * [[大山祇神]] ; 内神（習合時代の祭神） * [[船霊]]神 * [[猿田彦]]尊 * [[不動明王]] * [[十一面観音]] * [[烏天狗]] * [[飯綱権現]] * [[大日如来]] == 境内 == * 天狗彫刻 * 洞窟 ; 境内社 * [[荒神]]社 * 横浜[[弁財天]] * [[稲荷社]] * [[龍神社]] == 祭事 == * 1月 - [[歳旦祭]]、初金刀比羅祭 * 2月 - 稲荷祭 * 5月16日～17日 - 例大祭　 * 6月 - [[大祓]]夏越祭 * 7月 - [[七夕祭]] * 12月 - 十日納、金刀比羅祭、大祓除夜祭 == 交通アクセス == * [[京急]][[神奈川駅]]より徒歩3分。 * JR[[横浜駅]]西口より徒歩5分 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == *[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/ 大綱金刀比羅神社] - 公式サイト {{DEFAULTSORT:おおつなことひらしんしや}} [[Category:横浜市の神社]] [[Category:神奈川区の歴史]] [[Category:神奈川区の建築物]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧村社]] [[Category:金刀比羅神社]]

拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} |所在地 = [[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[神奈川区]][[台町 (横浜市)|台町]]7-34 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[大物主神]]、[[金山彦神]]、[[日本武尊]]、[[大山祇神]] |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 伝[[1180年]]（飯綱大権現/大綱神社）
[[1642年]]（金毘羅大権現）[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html 由緒] - 大綱金刀比羅神社 |本殿 = |別名 = 横浜のこんぴらさん[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 大綱金刀比羅神社] - 神奈川県神社庁 |札所等 = |例祭 = 5月16～17日 |神事 = }} '''大綱金刀比羅神社'''（おおつなことひらじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[神奈川区]][[台町 (横浜市)|台町]]にある[[神社]]。 == 概要 == 元々は山上（現・[[高島台]]）の「[[飯綱大権現]]」と麓の「[[金毘羅大権現]]」が別々に鎮座しており、前者には[[源頼朝]]にまつわる[[1180年]]（[[治承]]4年）の創建伝説が、後者には[[1642年]]（[[寛永]]19年）の[[棟札]]がある。 明治時代の神仏分離によってそれぞれ「大綱神社」「金刀比羅神社」となり、後者は[[1882年]]（明治15年）に村社に列せられる。[[1911年]]（明治44年）、前年の裏山崩壊によって半壊した金刀比羅神社の社殿再建を機に、大綱神社を合祀して「大綱金刀比羅神社」とする。 [[1985年]]（昭和60年）、裏山が崩壊して本殿・拝殿を喪失、神楽殿を「仮社殿」として現在に至る[https://dailyportalz.jp/kiji/shrine-with-tengu-and-cave-near-Yokohama-station 横浜駅から歩いて約10分 天狗と洞窟が迎える神社] - [[デイリーポータルZ]] 2022/11/29。 == 祭神 == * [[大物主神]] * [[金山彦神]] * [[日本武尊]] * [[大山祇神]] ; 内神（習合時代の祭神） * [[船霊]]神 * [[猿田彦]]尊 * [[不動明王]] * [[十一面観音]] * [[烏天狗]] * [[飯綱権現]] * [[大日如来]] == 境内 == * 天狗彫刻 * 洞窟 ; 境内社 * [[荒神]]社 * 横浜[[弁財天]] * [[稲荷社]] * [[龍神社]] == 祭事 == * 1月 - [[歳旦祭]]、初金刀比羅祭 * 2月 - 稲荷祭 * 5月16日～17日 - 例大祭　 * 6月 - [[大祓]]夏越祭 * 7月 - [[七夕祭]] * 12月 - 十日納、金刀比羅祭、大祓除夜祭 == 交通アクセス == * [[京急本線|京急]][[神奈川駅]]より徒歩3分。 * JR[[横浜駅]]西口より徒歩5分 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == *[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/ 大綱金刀比羅神社] - 公式サイト {{DEFAULTSORT:おおつなことひらしんしや}} [[Category:横浜市の神社]] [[Category:神奈川区の歴史]] [[Category:神奈川区の建築物]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧村社]] [[Category:金刀比羅神社]]

[[Category:Kk]][[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]]

大綱金刀比羅神社 [[Category:Kk]][[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Shrine | Name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | Image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | Location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | AlternateName = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} **Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine** (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. ### Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: **Iizuna Daigongen** (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and **Konpira Daigongen** (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed **Otsuna Shrine** and **Kotohira Shrine**, respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. ### Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] #### Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] ### Shrine Grounds * **Tengu Carvings** * **Cave** #### Sub-Shrines * **Aragami Shrine** * Yokohama **Benzaiten** * **Inari Shrine** * **Ryūjin Shrine** ### Annual Events * **January**: Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * **February**: Inari Festival * **May 16–17**: Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * **June**: Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * **July**: Tanabata Festival * **December**: Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) ### Access * **Keikyu Main Line**: 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * **JR Yokohama Station**: 5-minute walk from the west exit. ### References {{Reflist}} ### External Links * [http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/ Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Official Website {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Kanagawa Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Kanagawa Ward]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Kotohira Shrines]]

The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. ### Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] #### Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] ### Shrine Grounds * **Tengu Carvings** * **Cave** #### Sub-Shrines * **Aragami Shrine** * Yokohama **Benzaiten** * **Inari Shrine** * **Ryūjin Shrine** ### Annual Events * **January**: Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * **February**: Inari Festival * **May 16–17**: Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * **June**: Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * **July**: Tanabata Festival * **December**: Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) ### Access * **Keikyu Main Line**: 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * **JR Yokohama Station**: 5-minute walk from the west exit. ### References {{Reflist}} ### External Links * [http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/ Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - revision.text:
大綱金刀比羅神社 [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Shrine | Name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | Image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | Location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | AlternateName = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. ### Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. ### Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] #### Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] ### Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' #### Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' ### Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) ### Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. ### References {{Reflist}} ### External Links * 大綱金刀比羅神社 [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Shrine | Name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | Image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | Location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | AlternateName = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. ### Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. ### Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] #### Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] ### Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' #### Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' ### Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) ### Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. ### References {{Reflist}} ### External Links * revision.text:
大綱金刀比羅神社 [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Shrine | Name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | AlternateName = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. ### Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. ### Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] #### Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] ### Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' #### Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' ### Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) ### Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. ### References {{Reflist}} ### External Links * Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | AlternateName = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. ### Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. ### Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] #### Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] ### Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' #### Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' ### Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) ### Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. ### References {{Reflist}} ### External Links * 大綱金刀比羅神社 [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Shrine | Name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | AlternateName = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. ### Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. ### Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] #### Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] ### Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' #### Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' ### Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) ### Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. ### References {{Reflist}} ### External Links * revision.text:
大綱金刀比羅神社 [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Shrine | Name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | AlternateName = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. ### Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. ### Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] #### Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] ### Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' #### Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' ### Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) ### Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. ### References {{Reflist}} ### External Links * 大綱金刀比羅神社 [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Shrine | Name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | AlternateName = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. ### Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. ### Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] #### Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] ### Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' #### Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' ### Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) ### Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. ### References {{Reflist}} ### External Links * revision.text:
大綱金刀比羅神社 [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Shrine | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. ### Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. ### Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] #### Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] ### Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' #### Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' ### Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) ### Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. ### References {{Reflist}} ### External Links * {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Shrine | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. ### Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. ### Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] #### Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] ### Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' #### Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' ### Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) ### Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. ### References {{Reflist}} ### External Links * The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. ### Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] #### Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] ### Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' #### Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' ### Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) ### Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. ### References {{Reflist}} ### External Links * [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Shrine | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. ### Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. ### Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] #### Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] ### Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' #### Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' ### Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) ### Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. ### References {{Reflist}} ### External Links * Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Shrine | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. ### Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. ### Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] #### Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] ### Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' #### Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' ### Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) ### Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. ### References {{Reflist}} ### External Links * 大綱金刀比羅神社 [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]][[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]][[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]][[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Shrine | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. ### Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. ### Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] #### Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] ### Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' #### Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' ### Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) ### Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. ### References {{Reflist}} ### External Links * During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] ### Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' #### Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami [[Category:qq-Otsuna Konpira Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Shrine | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. ### Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. ### Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] #### Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] ### Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' #### Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' ### Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) ### Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. ### References {{Reflist}} ### External Links * [[Category:qq-Otsuna Konpira Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Shrine | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. ### Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. ### Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] #### Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] ### Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' #### Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' ### Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) ### Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. ### References {{Reflist}} ### External Links * | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. ### Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. ### Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] #### Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] ### Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' #### Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' ### Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) ### Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. ### References {{Reflist}} ### External Links * During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. ### Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] #### Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] ### Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' #### Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' ### Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) ### Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. ### References {{Reflist}} ### External Links * [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Otsuna Konpira Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Shrine | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. ### Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. ### Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] #### Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] ### Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' #### Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' ### Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) ### Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. ### References {{Reflist}} ### External Links * [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Otsuna Konpira Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Shrine | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. ### Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. ### Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] #### Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] ### Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' #### Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' ### Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) ### Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. ### References {{Reflist}} ### External Links * | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. ### Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. ### Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] #### Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] ### Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' #### Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' ### Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) ### Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. ### References {{Reflist}} ### External Links * [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Otsuna Konpira Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. ### Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. ### Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] #### Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] ### Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' #### Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' ### Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) ### Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. ### References {{Reflist}} ### External Links * [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Otsuna Konpira Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. ### Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. ### Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] #### Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] ### Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' #### Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' ### Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) ### Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. ### References {{Reflist}} ### External Links *
