= [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. ### Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. ### Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] #### Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] ### Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' #### Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' ### Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) ### Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. ### References {{Reflist}} ### External Links * [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Otsuna Konpira Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. ### Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. ### Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] #### Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] ### Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' #### Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' ### Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) ### Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. ### References {{Reflist}} ### External Links * [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Otsuna Konpira Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. ### Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. ### Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] #### Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] ### Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' #### Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' ### Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) ### Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. ### References {{Reflist}} ### External Links * = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ===# Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ===# Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Otsuna Konpira Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Otsuna Konpira Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * Shrine-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * [http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/ Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Official Website {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine}} [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] 大綱金刀比羅神社 [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]][[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]][[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]][[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * Konpira Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links *
