[[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15th year }}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15th year }}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60th year }}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15th year }}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60th year }}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15th year }}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60th year }}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15th year }}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60th year }}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}}[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15th year }}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60th year }}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * revision.text:
大綱金刀比羅神社 {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15th year }}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60th year }}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * [http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/ Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Official Website {{draft categories| [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Kanagawa Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Kanagawa Ward]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]] [[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] [[en:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine}}

During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離|12=draft}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15th year|12=draft}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60th year|12=draft}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * [http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/ Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Official Website {{draft categories| [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Kanagawa Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Kanagawa Ward]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]] [[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] [[en:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine}}

revision.text:
大綱金刀比羅神社 {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft|12=draft}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区|12=draft|12=draft}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承|12=draft|12=draft}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離|12=draft|12=draft}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * [http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/ Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Official Website {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Kanagawa Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Kanagawa Ward]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]] [[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] [[en:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine}}

revision.comment: 6 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:大綱金刀比羅神社 up to rev 101929351
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 大綱金刀比羅神社 |画像= [[FIle:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} |所在地 = [[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[神奈川区]][[台町 (横浜市)|台町]]7-34 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[大物主神]]、[[金山彦神]]、[[日本武尊]]、[[大山祇神]] |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 伝[[1180年]]（飯綱大権現/大綱神社）
[[1642年]]（金毘羅大権現）[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html 由緒] - 大綱金刀比羅神社 |本殿 = |別名 = 横浜のこんぴらさん[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 大綱金刀比羅神社] - 神奈川県神社庁 |札所等 = |例祭 = 5月16～17日 |神事 = }} '''大綱金刀比羅神社'''（おおつなことひらじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[神奈川区]][[台町 (横浜市)|台町]]にある[[神社]]。 == 概要 == 元々は山上（現・[[高島台]]）の「[[飯綱大権現]]」と麓の「[[金毘羅大権現]]」が別々に鎮座しており、前者には[[源頼朝]]にまつわる[[1180年]]（[[治承]]4年）の創建伝説が、後者には[[1642年]]（[[寛永]]19年）の[[棟札]]がある。 明治時代の神仏分離によってそれぞれ「大綱神社」「金刀比羅神社」となり、後者は[[1882年]]（明治15年）に村社に列せられる。[[1911年]]（明治44年）、前年の裏山崩壊によって半壊した金刀比羅神社の社殿再建を機に、大綱神社を合祀して「大綱金刀比羅神社」とする。 [[1985年]]（昭和60年）、裏山が崩壊して本殿・拝殿を喪失、神楽殿を「仮社殿」として現在に至る[https://dailyportalz.jp/kiji/shrine-with-tengu-and-cave-near-Yokohama-station 横浜駅から歩いて約10分 天狗と洞窟が迎える神社] - [[デイリーポータルZ]] 2022/11/29。 == 祭神 == * [[大物主神]] * [[金山彦神]] * [[日本武尊]] * [[大山祇神]] ; 内神（習合時代の祭神） * [[船霊]]神 * [[猿田彦]]尊 * [[不動明王]] * [[十一面観音]] * [[烏天狗]] * [[飯綱権現]] * [[大日如来]] == 境内 == * 天狗彫刻 * 洞窟 ; 境内社 * [[荒神]]社 * 横浜[[弁財天]] * [[稲荷社]] * [[龍神社]] == 祭事 == * 1月 - [[歳旦祭]]、初金刀比羅祭 * 2月 - 稲荷祭 * 5月16日～17日 - 例大祭　 * 6月 - [[大祓]]夏越祭 * 7月 - [[七夕祭]] * 12月 - 十日納、金刀比羅祭、大祓除夜祭 == 交通アクセス == * [[京急本線|京急]][[神奈川駅]]より徒歩3分。 * JR[[横浜駅]]西口より徒歩5分 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == *[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/ 大綱金刀比羅神社] - 公式サイト {{DEFAULTSORT:おおつなことひらしんしや}} [[Category:横浜市の神社]] [[Category:神奈川区の歴史]] [[Category:神奈川区の建築物]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧村社]] [[Category:金刀比羅神社]]

大綱金刀比羅神社 {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa Ward|ja|神奈川区}}, [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|1180|ja|治承}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|1642|ja|寛永}} with a plaque). During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15th year}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60th year}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko]] * [[Fudō Myōō]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * [http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/ Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Official Website {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Kanagawa Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Kanagawa Ward]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]] [[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] [[en:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otsuna Konpira Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|大綱金刀比羅神社|version=101929351|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within [[:en:Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama|Kanagawa Ward]], [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in [[:en:Jishō|1180]]) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in [[:en:Kan'ei|1642]] with a plaque). During the Meiji-era [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]], they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1882|qq=}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1985|qq=}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * [[Acala]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * [http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/ Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Official Website {{draft categories| {{draft categories| }} [[sn:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]] [[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] [[en:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:横浜市の神社]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within [[:en:Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama|Kanagawa Ward]], [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in [[:en:Jishō|1180]]) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in [[:en:Kan'ei|1642]] with a plaque). During the Meiji-era [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]], they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1882|qq=}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1985|qq=}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * [[Acala]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * [http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/ Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Official Website {{draft categories| {{draft categories| }} [[sn:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]] [[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] [[en:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:横浜市の神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧村社]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[Ōyamakui]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within [[:en:Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama|Kanagawa Ward]], [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in [[:en:Jishō|1180]]) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in [[:en:Kan'ei|1642]] with a plaque). During the Meiji-era [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]], they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|ja|明治15年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1882|qq=}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In {{ill|1985|ja|昭和60年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1985|qq=}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ōyamakui]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * [[Acala]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * [http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/ Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Official Website {{draft categories| {{draft categories| }} [[sn:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis but with jawiki links]][[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧村社]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Oyamakui no Kami]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within [[:en:Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama|Kanagawa Ward]], [[Yokohama]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in [[:en:Jishō|1180]]) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in [[:en:Kan'ei|1642]] with a plaque). During the Meiji-era [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]], they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In [[:en:1882|Meiji 15]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]], Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In [[:en:1985|Showa 60]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]], another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Oyamakui no Kami]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * [[Acala]] * [[Jūichimen Kannon]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * [http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/ Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Official Website {{draft categories| {{draft categories| }} [[sn:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]] [[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{translated page|ja|大綱金刀比羅神社|version=101929351|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]]

大綱金刀比羅神社 {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Ward|Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|Kanayamahiko]], [[Yamato Takeru]], [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Oyamakui no Kami]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within [[:en:Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama|Kanagawa Ward]], [[Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in [[:en:Jishō|1180]]) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in [[:en:Kan'ei|1642]] with a plaque). During the Meiji-era [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]], they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In [[:en:1882|Meiji 15]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]], Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In [[:en:1985|Showa 60]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]], another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|Kanayamahiko]] * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Oyamakui no Kami]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * [[Acala]] * [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Ekādaśamukha]] * [[Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[:en:Kanagawa Station|Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * [http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/ Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Official Website {{draft categories| {{draft categories| }} [[sn:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]] [[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages with translation template added]]{{translated page|ja|大綱金刀比羅神社|version=101929351|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[Category:横浜市の神社]] [[Category:神奈川区の建築物]] [[Category:神奈川区の歴史]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧村社]] [[Category:Kotohira_shrines]]

Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[Yokohama City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Ward|Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|Kanayamahiko]], [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]], [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Oyamakui no Kami]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within [[:en:Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama|Kanagawa Ward]], [[Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in [[:en:Jishō|1180]]) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in [[:en:Kan'ei|1642]] with a plaque). During the Meiji-era [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]], they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In [[:en:1882|Meiji 15]], Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In [[:en:1985|Showa 60]], another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|Kanayamahiko]] * [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] * [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Oyamakui no Kami]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * [[Acala]] * [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Ekādaśamukha]] * [[:en:Karasu Tengu|Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[:en:Kanagawa Station|Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * [http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/ Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Official Website {{draft categories| {{draft categories| }} [[sn:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]] [[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[sn:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]] [[Category:pages with translation template added]]{{translated page|ja|大綱金刀比羅神社|version=101929351|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[Category:横浜市の神社]] [[Category:神奈川区の建築物]] [[Category:神奈川区の歴史]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧村社]] [[Category:Kotohira_shrines]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Yokohama City|Yokohama City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Ward|Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|15.7|N|139|37|29.9|E|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|Kanayamahiko]], [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]], [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Oyamakui no Kami]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = }} '''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within [[:en:Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama|Kanagawa Ward]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. === Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in [[:en:Jishō|1180]]) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in [[:en:Kan'ei|1642]] with a plaque). During the Meiji-era [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]], they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In [[:en:1882|Meiji 15]], Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall. In [[:en:1985|Showa 60]], another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine. === Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|Kanayamahiko]] * [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] * [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Oyamakui no Kami]] ==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * [[Acala]] * [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Ekādaśamukha]] * [[:en:Karasu Tengu|Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[:en:Vairocana|Vairocana]] === Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave''' ==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine''' === Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya) === Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[:en:Kanagawa Station|Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit. === References {{Reflist}} === External Links * [http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/ Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Official Website {{draft categories| {{draft categories| }} [[sn:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]] [[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[sn:Otsuna Konpira Shrine]] [[Category:pages with translation template added]]{{translated page|ja|大綱金刀比羅神社|version=101929351|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[Category:横浜市の神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Kanagawa Ward]] [[Category:神奈川区の歴史]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kotohira_shrines]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg | location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Yokohama City|Yokohama City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Ward|Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{Coord|35.47102777777778|N|139.62497222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|Kanayamahiko]], [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]], [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Oyamakui no Kami]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)<ref name="yuisho">[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine</ref> | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san<ref name="jinjacho">[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau</ref> | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = | prefecture = {{ill|Dai-machi|qid=Q22124260}}; {{ill|Kanagawa-ku|qid=Q1143712}} }}
'''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within [[:en:Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama|Kanagawa Ward]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]].
=== Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in [[:en:Jishō|1180]]) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in [[:en:Kan'ei|1642]] with a plaque).<ref name="jinjacho"/> During the Meiji-era [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]], they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In [[:en:1882|Meiji 15]], Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall.<ref name="jinjacho"/>
In [[:en:1985|Showa 60]], another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine.<ref name="jinjacho"/>
=== Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|Kanayamahiko]] * [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] * [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Oyamakui no Kami]]
==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * [[Acala]] * [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Ekādaśamukha]] * [[:en:Karasu Tengu|Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[:en:Vairocana|Vairocana]]
=== Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave'''
==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine'''
=== Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya)
=== Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[:en:Kanagawa Station|Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit.
=== References {{Reflist}}
=== External Links * [http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/ Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Official Website

In [[:en:1985|Showa 60]], another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine.<ref name="jinjacho"/>

==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * [[Acala]] * [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Ekādaśamukha]] * [[:en:Karasu Tengu|Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[:en:Vairocana|Vairocana]]
=== Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave'''
==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine'''
=== Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya)
=== Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[:en:Kanagawa Station|Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg | location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Yokohama City|Yokohama City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Ward|Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{Coord|35.47102777777778|N|139.62497222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|Kanayamahiko]], [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]], [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Oyamakui no Kami]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)<ref name="yuisho">[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine</ref> | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san<ref name="jinjacho">[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau</ref> | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Dai-machi|qid=Q22124260}}; {{ill|Kanagawa-ku|qid=Q1143712}} }}
'''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within [[:en:Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama|Kanagawa Ward]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]].
=== Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in [[:en:Jishō|1180]]) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in [[:en:Kan'ei|1642]] with a plaque).<ref name="jinjacho"/> During the Meiji-era [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]], they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In [[:en:1882|Meiji 15]], Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall.<ref name="jinjacho"/>
In [[:en:1985|Showa 60]], another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine.<ref name="jinjacho"/>
=== Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|Kanayamahiko]] * [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] * [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Oyamakui no Kami]]
==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * [[Acala]] * [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Ekādaśamukha]] * [[:en:Karasu Tengu|Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[:en:Vairocana|Vairocana]]
=== Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave'''
==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine'''
=== Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya)
=== Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[:en:Kanagawa Station|Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit.
=== References {{Reflist}}
=== External Links * [http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/ Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Official Website
