<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q117062765]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} 大綱金刀比羅神社
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg | location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Yokohama City|Yokohama City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Ward|Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{Coord|35.47102777777778|N|139.62497222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|Kanayamahiko]], [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]], [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Oyamakui no Kami]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)<ref name="yuisho">[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine</ref> | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san<ref name="jinjacho">[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau</ref> | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Dai-machi|qid=Q22124260}}; {{ill|Kanagawa-ku|qid=Q1143712}} }}
'''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within [[:en:Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama|Kanagawa Ward]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]].
=== Overview Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in [[:en:Jishō|1180]]) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in [[:en:Kan'ei|1642]] with a plaque).<ref name="jinjacho"/> During the Meiji-era [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]], they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In [[:en:1882|Meiji 15]], Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall.<ref name="jinjacho"/>
In [[:en:1985|Showa 60]], another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine.<ref name="jinjacho"/>
=== Deities * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|Kanayamahiko]] * [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] * [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Oyamakui no Kami]]
==== Syncretic (Historical) Deities * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * [[Acala]] * [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Ekādaśamukha]] * [[:en:Karasu Tengu|Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[:en:Vairocana|Vairocana]]
=== Shrine Grounds * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave'''
==== Sub-Shrines * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine'''
=== Annual Events * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya)
=== Access * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[:en:Kanagawa Station|Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit.
=== References {{Reflist}}
=== External Links * [http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/ Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Official Website

In [[:en:1985|Showa 60]], another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine.<ref name="jinjacho"/>

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg | location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Yokohama City|Yokohama City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Ward|Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{Coord|35.47102777777778|N|139.62497222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|Kanayamahiko]], [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]], [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Oyamakui no Kami]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)<ref name="yuisho">[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine</ref> | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san<ref name="jinjacho">[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau</ref> | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Dai-machi|WD=Q22124260}}; {{ill|Kanagawa-ku|WD=Q1143712}} }}
'''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within [[:en:Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama|Kanagawa Ward]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]].
== Overview == Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in [[:en:Jishō|1180]]) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in [[:en:Kan'ei|1642]] with a plaque).<ref name="jinjacho"/> During the Meiji-era [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]], they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In [[:en:1882|Meiji 15]], Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall.<ref name="jinjacho"/>
In [[:en:1985|Showa 60]], another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine.<ref name="jinjacho"/>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|Kanayamahiko]] * [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] * [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Oyamakui no Kami]]
=== Syncretic (Historical) Deities === * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * [[Acala]] * [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Ekādaśamukha]] * [[:en:Karasu Tengu|Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[:en:Vairocana|Vairocana]]
== Shrine Grounds == * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave'''
=== Sub-Shrines === * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine'''
== Annual Events == * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya)
== Access == * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[:en:Kanagawa Station|Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/ Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Official Website

In [[:en:1985|Showa 60]], another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine.<ref name="jinjacho"/>

=== Syncretic (Historical) Deities === * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * [[Acala]] * [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Ekādaśamukha]] * [[:en:Karasu Tengu|Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[:en:Vairocana|Vairocana]]
== Shrine Grounds == * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave'''
=== Sub-Shrines === * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine'''
== Annual Events == * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya)
== Access == * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[:en:Kanagawa Station|Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg | location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Yokohama City|Yokohama City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Ward|Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{Coord|35.47102777777778|N|139.62497222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|Kanayamahiko]], [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]], [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Oyamakui no Kami]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)<ref name="yuisho">[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine</ref> | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san<ref name="jinjacho">[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau</ref> | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Dai-machi|WD=Q22124260}}; {{ill|Kanagawa-ku|WD=Q1143712}} }}
'''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within [[:en:Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama|Kanagawa Ward]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]].
== Overview == Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in [[:en:Jishō|1180]]) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in [[:en:Kan'ei|1642]] with a plaque).<ref name="jinjacho"/> During the Meiji-era [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]], they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In [[:en:1882|Meiji 15]], Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall.<ref name="jinjacho"/>
In [[:en:1985|Showa 60]], another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine.<ref name="jinjacho"/>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|Kanayamahiko]] * [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] * [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Oyamakui no Kami]]
=== Syncretic (Historical) Deities === * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * [[Acala]] * [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Ekādaśamukha]] * [[:en:Karasu Tengu|Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[:en:Vairocana|Vairocana]]
== Shrine Grounds == * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave'''
=== Sub-Shrines === * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine'''
== Annual Events == * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya)
== Access == * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[:en:Kanagawa Station|Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/ Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Official Website

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q117062765]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} 大綱金刀比羅神社
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg | location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Yokohama City|Yokohama City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Ward|Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{Coord|35.47102777777778|N|139.62497222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|Kanayamahiko]], [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]], [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Oyamakui no Kami]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)<ref name="yuisho">[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine</ref> | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san<ref name="jinjacho">[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau</ref> | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Dai-machi|WD=Q22124260}}; {{ill|Kanagawa-ku|WD=Q1143712}} }}
'''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within [[:en:Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama|Kanagawa Ward]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]].
== Overview == Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in [[:en:Jishō|1180]]) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in [[:en:Kan'ei|1642]] with a plaque).<ref name="jinjacho"/> During the Meiji-era [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]], they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In [[:en:1882|Meiji 15]], Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall.<ref name="jinjacho"/>
In [[:en:1985|Showa 60]], another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine.<ref name="jinjacho"/>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|Kanayamahiko]] * [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] * [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Oyamakui no Kami]]
=== Syncretic (Historical) Deities === * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * [[Acala]] * [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Ekādaśamukha]] * [[:en:Karasu Tengu|Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[:en:Vairocana|Vairocana]]
== Shrine Grounds == * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave'''
=== Sub-Shrines === * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine'''
== Annual Events == * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya)
== Access == * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[:en:Kanagawa Station|Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/ Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Official Website

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q117062765]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} 大綱金刀比羅神社
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg | location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Yokohama City|Yokohama City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Ward|Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{Coord|35.47102777777778|N|139.62497222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|Kanayamahiko]], [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]], [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Oyamakui no Kami]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)<ref name="yuisho">[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine</ref> | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san<ref name="jinjacho">[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau</ref> | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Dai-machi|WD=Q22124260}}; {{ill|Kanagawa-ku|WD=Q1143712}} }}
'''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within [[:en:Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama|Kanagawa Ward]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]].
== Overview == Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in [[:en:Jishō|1180]]) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in [[:en:Kan'ei|1642]] with a plaque).<ref name="jinjacho"/> During the Meiji-era [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]], they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In [[:en:1882|Meiji 15]], Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall.<ref name="jinjacho"/>
In [[:en:1985|Showa 60]], another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine.<ref name="jinjacho"/>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|Kanayamahiko]] * [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] * [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Oyamakui no Kami]]
=== Syncretic (Historical) Deities === * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * [[Acala]] * [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Ekādaśamukha]] * [[:en:Karasu Tengu|Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[:en:Vairocana|Vairocana]]
== Shrine Grounds == * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave'''
=== Sub-Shrines === * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine'''
== Annual Events == * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya)
== Access == * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[:en:Kanagawa Station|Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/ Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Official Website

'''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within [[:en:Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama|Kanagawa Ward]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]].
== Overview == Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in [[:en:Jishō|1180]]) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in [[:en:Kan'ei|1642]] with a plaque).<ref name="jinjacho"/> During the Meiji-era [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu Bunri]], they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In [[:en:1882|Meiji 15]], Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall.<ref name="jinjacho"/>
In [[:en:1985|Showa 60]], another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine.<ref name="jinjacho"/>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|Kanayamahiko]] * [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] * [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Oyamakui no Kami]]
=== Syncretic (Historical) Deities === * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * [[Acala]] * [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Ekādaśamukha]] * [[:en:Karasu Tengu|Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * [[:en:Vairocana|Vairocana]]
== Shrine Grounds == * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave'''
=== Sub-Shrines === * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine'''
== Annual Events == * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya)
== Access == * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from [[:en:Kanagawa Station|Kanagawa Station]]. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/ Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Official Website

'''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama|en|Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama|WD=Q1143712|lt=Kanagawa Ward}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|WD=Q38283|lt=Yokohama}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}.
== Overview == Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|Jishō|en|Jishō|WD=Q1194671|lt=1180}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=1642}} with a plaque).<ref name="jinjacho"/> During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|en|1882|WD=Q7812|lt=Meiji 15}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall.<ref name="jinjacho"/>
In {{ill|1985|en|1985|WD=Q2431|lt=Showa 60}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine.<ref name="jinjacho"/>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|Kanayamahiko]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru}} * {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|en|Oyamakui no Kami|WD=Q10935105|lt=Oyamakui no Kami}}
=== Syncretic (Historical) Deities === * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * [[Acala]] * {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Ekādaśamukha}} * [[:en:Karasu Tengu|Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * {{ill|Vairocana|en|Vairocana|WD=Q239847|lt=Vairocana}}
== Shrine Grounds == * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave'''
=== Sub-Shrines === * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine'''
== Annual Events == * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya)
== Access == * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from {{ill|Kanagawa Station|en|Kanagawa Station|WD=Q6360285|lt=Kanagawa Station}}. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/ Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Official Website

大綱金刀比羅神社
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg | location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, [[:en:Yokohama City|Yokohama City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Ward|Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{Coord|35.47102777777778|N|139.62497222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|Kanayamahiko]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru}}, {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|en|Oyamakui no Kami|WD=Q10935105|lt=Oyamakui no Kami}} | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)<ref name="yuisho">[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine</ref> | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san<ref name="jinjacho">[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau</ref> | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Dai-machi|WD=Q22124260}}; {{ill|Kanagawa-ku|WD=Q1143712}} }}
'''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama|en|Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama|WD=Q1143712|lt=Kanagawa Ward}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|WD=Q38283|lt=Yokohama}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}.
== Overview == Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|Jishō|en|Jishō|WD=Q1194671|lt=1180}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=1642}} with a plaque).<ref name="jinjacho"/> During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|en|1882|WD=Q7812|lt=Meiji 15}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall.<ref name="jinjacho"/>
In {{ill|1985|en|1985|WD=Q2431|lt=Showa 60}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine.<ref name="jinjacho"/>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|Kanayamahiko]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru}} * {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|en|Oyamakui no Kami|WD=Q10935105|lt=Oyamakui no Kami}}
=== Syncretic (Historical) Deities === * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * [[Acala]] * {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Ekādaśamukha}} * [[:en:Karasu Tengu|Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * {{ill|Vairocana|en|Vairocana|WD=Q239847|lt=Vairocana}}
== Shrine Grounds == * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave'''
=== Sub-Shrines === * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine'''
== Annual Events == * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya)
== Access == * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from {{ill|Kanagawa Station|en|Kanagawa Station|WD=Q6360285|lt=Kanagawa Station}}. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/ Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Official Website

大綱金刀比羅神社
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg | location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, [[:en:Yokohama City|Yokohama City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Ward|Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{Coord|35.47102777777778|N|139.62497222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|Kanayamahiko]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}, {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|en|Oyamakui no Kami|WD=Q10935105|lt=Oyamakui no Kami|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)<ref name="yuisho">[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine</ref> | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san<ref name="jinjacho">[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau</ref> | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Dai-machi|WD=Q22124260|1=Dai-machi}}; {{ill|Kanagawa-ku|WD=Q1143712|1=Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama}} }}
'''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama|en|Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama|WD=Q1143712|lt=Kanagawa Ward|1=Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|WD=Q38283|lt=Yokohama|1=Yokohama}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}.
== Overview == Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|Jishō|en|Jishō|WD=Q1194671|lt=1180|1=Jishō}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=1642|1=Kan'ei}} with a plaque).<ref name="jinjacho"/> During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|en|1882|WD=Q7812|lt=Meiji 15|1=1882}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall.<ref name="jinjacho"/>
In {{ill|1985|en|1985|WD=Q2431|lt=Showa 60|1=1985}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine.<ref name="jinjacho"/>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|Kanayamahiko]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}} * {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|en|Oyamakui no Kami|WD=Q10935105|lt=Oyamakui no Kami|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}
=== Syncretic (Historical) Deities === * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * [[Acala]] * {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Ekādaśamukha|1=Ekādaśamukha}} * [[:en:Karasu Tengu|Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * {{ill|Vairocana|en|Vairocana|WD=Q239847|lt=Vairocana|1=Vairocana}}
== Shrine Grounds == * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave'''
=== Sub-Shrines === * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine'''
== Annual Events == * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya)
== Access == * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from {{ill|Kanagawa Station|en|Kanagawa Station|WD=Q6360285|lt=Kanagawa Station|1=Kanagawa Station}}. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/ Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Official Website

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大綱金刀比羅神社]]
大綱金刀比羅神社
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine (大綱金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Yokohama Kompira.jpg | location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, [[:en:Yokohama City|Yokohama City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Ward|Kanagawa Ward]], [[Taimachi|Taimachi]] 7-34 | coordinates = {{Coord|35.47102777777778|N|139.62497222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|Kanayamahiko]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}, {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|en|Oyamakui no Kami|WD=Q10935105|lt=Oyamakui no Kami|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]] | Established = Reportedly [[1180]] (for Iizuna Daigongen/Otsuna Shrine); [[1642]] (for Konpira Daigongen)<ref name="yuisho">[http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/2.html History] - Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine</ref> | MainHall = | Alternatename = Yokohama's Kompira-san<ref name="jinjacho">[https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=132&cd=1202037 Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Kanagawa Shrine Bureau</ref> | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = May 16–17 | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Dai-machi|WD=Q22124260|1=Dai-machi}}; {{ill|Kanagawa-ku|WD=Q1143712|1=Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama}} }}
'''Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine''' (大綱金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} shrine located in [[Taimachi]] within {{ill|Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama|en|Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama|WD=Q1143712|lt=Kanagawa Ward|1=Kanagawa-ku, Yokohama}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|WD=Q38283|lt=Yokohama|1=Yokohama}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}.
== Overview == Originally, two separate shrines existed: '''Iizuna Daigongen''' (on the hill, established in {{ill|Jishō|en|Jishō|WD=Q1194671|lt=1180|1=Jishō}}) and '''Konpira Daigongen''' (at the foot, built in {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=1642|1=Kan'ei}} with a plaque).<ref name="jinjacho"/> During the Meiji-era {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, they were renamed '''Otsuna Shrine''' and '''Kotohira Shrine''', respectively. In {{ill|1882|en|1882|WD=Q7812|lt=Meiji 15|1=1882}}, Kotohira Shrine received the village shrine designation. In 1911 (Meiji 44), the two were merged under the current name after a landslide destroyed the Kotohira shrine hall.<ref name="jinjacho"/>
In {{ill|1985|en|1985|WD=Q2431|lt=Showa 60|1=1985}}, another landslide destroyed the shrine's main and worship halls. The kagura hall has since served as a temporary shrine.<ref name="jinjacho"/>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi]] * [[Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|Kanayamahiko]] * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}} * {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|en|Oyamakui no Kami|WD=Q10935105|lt=Oyamakui no Kami|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}
=== Syncretic (Historical) Deities === * [[Funadama]] * [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] * [[Acala]] * {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Ekādaśamukha|1=Ekādaśamukha}} * [[:en:Karasu Tengu|Karasu Tengu]] * [[Iizuna Gongen]] * {{ill|Vairocana|en|Vairocana|WD=Q239847|lt=Vairocana|1=Vairocana}}
== Shrine Grounds == * '''Tengu Carvings''' * '''Cave'''
=== Sub-Shrines === * '''Aragami Shrine''' * Yokohama '''Benzaiten''' * '''Inari Shrine''' * '''Ryūjin Shrine'''
== Annual Events == * '''January''': Saitan Festival, First Kotohira Festival * '''February''': Inari Festival * '''May 16–17''': Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * '''June''': Natsukoshi (Summer) Purification * '''July''': Tanabata Festival * '''December''': Year-End Festivals (Tōka-nohō, Kotohira Festival, Ōharae Joya)
== Access == * '''Keikyu Main Line''': 3-minute walk from {{ill|Kanagawa Station|en|Kanagawa Station|WD=Q6360285|lt=Kanagawa Station|1=Kanagawa Station}}. * '''JR Yokohama Station''': 5-minute walk from the west exit.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.yokohamakonpira.jp/ Ōtsuna Kotohira Shrine] - Official Website
